text: : سَأَلَهُ سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ وَ أَنَا مَعَهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ سِتَّةَ أَشْوَاطٍ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «وَ كَيْفَ يَطُوفُ سِتَّةَ أَشْوَاطٍ» فَقَالَ اِسْتَقْبَلَ اَلْحَجَرَ فَقَالَ اَللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ وَ عَقَدَ وَاحِداً فَقَالَ «يَطُوفُ شَوْطاً» قَالَ سُلَيْمَانُ فَإِنْ فَاتَهُ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى أَتَى أَهْلَهُ قَالَ «يَأْمُرُ مَنْ يَطُوفُ عَنْهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2803 - It is narrated from al-Hasan ibn Atiyyah, who said: Sulayman ibn Khalid, while I was with him, asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who performed six rounds of tawaf around the Kaaba.
Abu Abdullah (as) said: "How does he perform six rounds?"
Sulayman replied: "He faced the Black Stone, said 'Allahu Akbar,' and counted one."
Imam (as) said: "He should complete one more round."
Sulayman then asked: "What if he missed this and returned to his family?"
Imam (as) said: "He should arrange for someone to perform the remaining round on his behalf."
